title.en: Oh, come on! Seriously! 아, 쫌! 아, 정말!

description.en: When a friend won't stop joking! Learn reactions used in frustrating or annoying situations. Feel the subtle nuance of "A, jjom!"
description.ko: 친구가 장난을 멈추지 않을 때! 답답하거나 짜증 나는 상황에서 쓰는 리액션들을 배워봅니다. "아, 쫌!"의 미묘한 뉘앙스를 느껴보세요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 즐겁게 시작해 볼까요? 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! Shall we start fun today? I'm Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. Look at me. I can balance this stapler on my nose. Watch!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨. 지금 녹음 중이잖아요.
content.translation: John. We are recording right now.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oops. It fell. Let me try again. One..., two...,

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: Oh. Come on!

insight.title: 아 쫌
insight.description: A very common way to say 'Stop it' or 'Come on'
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone is being annoying or persistent. 'Jjom' originally means 'a little'. but here it's an exclamation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아..., 정말!" 존 씨. 공부합시다!
content.translation: Seriously! John. Let's study!

insight.title: 아 정말
insight.usage_tip: Used to express frustration when things don't go your way.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. Okay. I'll stop. Today's reactions are perfect for this situation. Right.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "아~ 쫌!" I love how it sounds. It's so short but powerful.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "아 쫌"은 상대방이 귀찮게 할 때..., "그만해" 라는 뜻으로 제일 많이 써요.
content.translation: Exactly. 'A jjom' is most often used to mean 'Stop it' when someone is bothering you.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about "아 진짜?". It sounds a bit stronger.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "아~ 진짜!" 는 짜증이 조금 더 섞인 느낌이에요.
content.translation: Right! 'A~ jinjja!' feels like it has a bit more annoyance mixed in.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Oh. For real...,

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 왜 그런 행동을 하는지 물어볼 때는 "왜 그래~!" 라고 해요.
content.translation: And when you ask why someone is acting that way. Say 'Wae geurae~!'.

insight.title: 왜 그래
insight.usage_tip: Can be used as a complaint or a genuine question of concern.

content.speaker: John
content.text: So it's like "Why are you doing that."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 맞아요. "도대체 왜 그래요~!" 라고 하면 더 강조가 되겠죠?
content.translation: Yes. That's right. If you say 'Do-dae-che wae geurae-yo~!' it adds more emphasis. right?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. Can I have a bite of your sandwich. Just one bite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니요. 아까 제 과자 다 드셨잖아요.
content.translation: No. You ate all my snacks earlier.

content.speaker: John
content.text: But it looks so good. Just a tiny tiny bite. Please.

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: Oh. Come on!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Just the corner. I promise.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아..., 정말! 왜 그래요~!"
content.translation: Seriously! Why are you being like this~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm just so hungry.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아~ 진짜!" 존 씨. 아까 점심 많이 먹었잖아요!
content.translation: Argh. For real! John. You had a big lunch earlier!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Hahaha. I'm just joking. I already had my sandwich.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아..., 진짜!" 존 씨 때문에 못 살아요 정말!
content.translation: Oh. For real! I can't live because of you. Seriously!

insight.title: 못 살아
insight.usage_tip: A common idiom used when someone is being too much or making you exhausted in a funny or frustrating way.

content.speaker: John
content.text: These expressions are so fun to use when my friends tease me.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 하지만 너무 자주 쓰면 친구가 슬퍼할 수도 있으니까 조심하세요!
content.translation: Exactly. But be careful because your friend might get sad if you use them too often!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "아~ 쫌!"
content.translation: Oh. Come on!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "아..., 정말!"
content.translation: Seriously!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "왜 그래~!"
content.translation: Why are you being like this~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "아~ 진짜!"
content.translation: Argh. For real!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "아..., 진짜 못 살아!"
content.translation: Oh. I can't live!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가 장난을 치면 오늘 배운 표현으로 답해 보세요!
content.translation: Everyone. If someone pranks you. Try responding with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 아 쫌! 그만 좀 하세요. (Oh come on! Please stop it.)
갑자기 나한테 왜 그래? (Why are you doing this to me all of a sudden?)
와. 진짜 맛있겠다. (Wow. It looks truly delicious.)
아이고. 내가 너 때문에 못 살아. (Goodness. I can't live because of you.)
